concept.title: GUIDES-42533: Nested keydefs (Map → Map → Topic) not resolved in Author (IBM DX)
concept.shortdesc: With Map A as context, keyrefs that depend on definitions in an intermediate keymap (Map B) and a keyword topic (Topic C) stay unresolved in AEM Guides Web Editor; DITA-OT publishing resolves them.

section.title: Problem
section.p: Authors use a root map (Map A) that keydefs a secondary map (Map B). Map B defines product keys and points at a topic (Topic C) for keyword values. A consuming topic (Topic D) uses keyword keyref for those keys. In Guides Author/Preview, keys from Map B / Topic C do not resolve when Map A is the opened context map, unless Map B is opened as the root context. Published HTML5 output from DITA-OT resolves all keys.

ul.li: Tenant: IBM DX Platforms — Cloud Manager cm-p132321-e1283790 (prod).

section.title: Reproduction outline

ol.li: Map A: keydef to Map B (format="ditamap") plus topicref to Topic D.
Map B: inline keydef for product name; keydef to Topic C; keys for version text (see bundled sample files).
Topic C: keyword id for version string (prolog metadata).
Topic D: body uses keyword keyref="productName" and keyword keyref="versionString".
Open Map A as context in Web Editor: observe unresolved keyrefs in Topic D. Open Map B as context: keyrefs may resolve (workaround).

section.note: Engineering comment on the ticket notes the written steps are confusing and asks for a clearer repro or customer content; use the sample maps/topics in this folder as a minimal aligned set.

strow.stentry: Recursive keydef loading: keys from nested keymaps resolve when the outer map is context, consistent with DITA-OT.
Nested keydefs from Map B (and Topic C) not applied in Author/Preview when Map A is context; no errors — placeholders stay unresolved.

section.title: Bundled minimal sample (this dataset)
section.p: Jira Representative Sample used <keywords> as a pseudo-root; Topic C was normalized to a valid <topic> with prolog/metadata/keywords and a keydef to #versionString in Map B.

section.title: Links
section.p: Author URL (VPN/SAML): sample link in Jira; long query-string editor URL omitted here — see issue description.
